title: Unforgettable Brazilian Cheese Pastry
description: A nostalgic and cultured Brazilian dish featuring a soured cream and chive dip, blanch, and cheese-pull. Perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, salt, and pepper.
Add the softened butter to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch (6 mm).
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a spoonful of the soured cream mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the pastries with the beaten egg and sprinkle with grated cheese.
Place the pastries on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes.
While the pastries are baking, prepare the chive dip by mixing together the soured cream, chopped chives, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Once the pastries are golden brown, remove them from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Serve the pastries warm with the chive dip on the side.
